---
title: "{{TITLE}}"
subtitle: "Preliminary Investigative Report"
author: "Opto-Mechanical Engineering Group"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
document-type: PIR
project-code: "{{XX}}"
status: draft
keywords: []
---

<!-- ============================================================
     PIR Template — Preliminary Investigative Report

     Purpose: Exploratory analysis and modeling to establish what
     is happening and why. Produces traceable findings (FND-XX-nnn)
     that drive requirements in a downstream EPS.

     Traceability: Each finding uses a Quarto anchor {#fnd-xx-nnn}
     so downstream documents can cross-reference with @fnd-xx-nnn.
     ============================================================ -->

# Summary {#sec-summary}

<!--
Brief overview of the investigation scope and key findings.
Write last, after the findings are established.
-->

# System Description {#sec-system}

<!--
What is being investigated. Physical description, operating context,
interfaces. Enough for a reader unfamiliar with the hardware to
follow the analysis.
-->

# Operating Envelope {#sec-envelope}

<!--
The boundary conditions: temperatures, pressures, durations, loads,
environments. Presented as a summary table with basis/source for
each parameter.
-->

# Model {#sec-model}

<!--
The analytical or computational model used to investigate the system.
Governing equations, assumptions, simplifications. Executable Python
cells produce figures directly.
-->

## Assumptions

<!--
Numbered list. Each assumption states what is assumed and why.
Flag assumptions that materially affect the findings.
-->

## Governing Equations

## Results

# Findings {#sec-findings}

<!--
Each finding is a discrete, traceable statement of fact established
by the investigation. Use the format below. Disposition is set when
the downstream EPS is written — leave as TBD in the PIR.

::: {#fnd-xx-001}
**FND-XX-001:** [Statement of finding.]
*Disposition: TBD*
:::
-->

# Conclusions {#sec-conclusions}

<!--
Summary of what the investigation established. No design solutions —
those belong in the EDS. If the findings point toward a clear next
action, state it here.
-->
